title: Reclaim Leadership Time at NEXT.io
problem: NEXT.io is using you as the executional safety net, which steals time from pricing, forecasting, GTM, leadership and Strait Up Growth.
cause: Executional work has no clean intake, ownership, standard, escalation path, or automation route, so it defaults back to you.
why_from_answers: You said you get pulled into HubSpot imports, segmented lists, forms, direct fixes, sales collateral and middleman communication. You said you want to stop being the go-to person for executional tasks and become the go-to person for commercial strategy, business growth, change management and operational efficiency.
why_it_matters: This is not a productivity reflection exercise. It is the input layer for future delegation, scheduled task builds, Claude Cowork workflows, live artifacts, and automations. If a task keeps landing on you, it must become owned, standardised, automated, scheduled, or killed.
minimum_version: Pick 5 executional tasks you currently absorb. For each, name the correct owner, the standard, the redirect script, and whether it should become a future task, scheduled task, live artifact, or automation candidate.
full_version: A Leadership Time Reclaim Register with five sections: stop doing, delegate, supervise by standard, automate later, and keep. Each row must have owner, standard, redirect script, escalation path, future build route, and reusable output.
use_asset_reason: Open only the smallest relevant asset pack for this task. Do not dump the whole knowledge base into the model.
human_first_action: Open a blank table. List every executional task you did or chased this week. For each one, write who should own it, what good looks like, and whether the repeatable part should become a scheduled reminder, Claude Cowork task, live artifact, or automation candidate.
do_not_ask_ai_until: Write the rough human version first, or list the live facts and constraints. Then use AI to structure, challenge, sharpen or source-check.
output: A Leadership Time Reclaim Register that becomes source material for future roadmap tasks, scheduled tasks, live artifacts, and automations.
success_test: At least 5 tasks classified, 1 task redirected this week, and at least 2 future build candidates captured for the roadmap.
kill_rule: If the output is only a complaint list, kill it. If it does not create owner, standard, script, escalation and future-build route, redo it.
friday_review_question: Which executional task did I stop owning, and which repeated task should become a scheduled task, live artifact, or automation candidate?
privacy_warning: Use anonymised examples. Do not paste confidential personal data, client data, employee performance details, compensation data, emails, CRM exports or sensitive commercial information into any external tool unless approved and safe.
original_action: Build a Leadership Time Reclaim Register that feeds delegation, future roadmap tasks, scheduled task builds, live artifacts and automations.

downstream_outputs.name: Stop Doing Register
downstream_outputs.use: Defines tasks Stuart will no longer execute.

downstream_outputs.name: Ownership Map
downstream_outputs.use: Feeds owner-to-owner communication, Jo task briefs, Monday.com task ownership and escalation.

downstream_outputs.name: Boundary Script Bank
downstream_outputs.use: Feeds future coaching prompts, email drafts and leadership scripts.

downstream_outputs.name: Scheduled Task Candidates
downstream_outputs.use: Feeds ChatGPT Tasks or Claude scheduled reminders for recurring checks, reviews and follow-ups.

downstream_outputs.name: Live Artifact Candidates
downstream_outputs.use: Feeds Claude Artifacts or HTML tools for trackers, forms, dashboards or scorecards.

downstream_outputs.name: Automation Candidates
downstream_outputs.use: Feeds Make, Zapier, n8n, Claude Cowork or ChatGPT Agent specs after the manual workflow proves useful.

feeds_into.label: Follow-Up and Accountability Tracker
feeds_into.reason: Tasks with owners and deadlines become tracked follow-ups.

feeds_into.label: Ownership Message to Jo
feeds_into.reason: CRM execution tasks become written ownership and performance expectations.

feeds_into.label: Owner-to-Owner Communication Rule
feeds_into.reason: Middleman tasks become direct owner communication rules.

feeds_into.label: Manual Follow-Up System
feeds_into.reason: Repeated follow-ups become manual workflow candidates.

feeds_into.label: Automation Only After Manual Proof
feeds_into.reason: Repeatable tasks become automation candidates only after manual proof.

future_build_rules: Scheduled task candidate: use when the work is recurring, time-based, and mostly a reminder or review.
Claude Cowork candidate: use when the work needs file, folder, document or Microsoft 365 context.
Live artifact candidate: use when the work needs an interactive checklist, tracker, calculator, prompt runner or scorecard.
Automation candidate: use only after the manual workflow has been used at least twice and still saves time.
Do not automate vague ownership. First define owner, standard, deadline and escalation.

title: Build the Follow-Up and Accountability Tracker
cause: Requests, deadlines and blockers are scattered across memory, email, Teams and meetings.
why_from_answers: You said chasing approvals, decisions, and missing information is one of the highest-friction workflows an agent could absorb. You also said you chase too much from memory.
why_it_matters: You said chasing approvals, information and decisions drains you. You are chasing from memory.
minimum_version: Create the five columns you will use today and add three live items.
full_version: One tracker showing who owes what, by when, what is blocked and what message to send next.
use_asset_reason: Open only the smallest relevant asset pack for this task. Do not dump the whole knowledge base into the model.
human_first_action: Create columns: owner, ask, deadline, blocked decision, risk, last chase, next chase, escalation date, status. Add the five most urgent open asks.
do_not_ask_ai_until: Write the rough human version first, or list the live facts and constraints. Then use AI to structure, challenge, sharpen or source-check.
output: One tracker showing who owes what, by when, what is blocked and what message to send next.
success_test: Tracker used 4 times. One blocked decision becomes visible.
kill_rule: If it takes more than 5 minutes a day, simplify it.
friday_review_question: What did I stop carrying in my head?
privacy_warning: Use anonymised examples. Do not paste confidential personal data, client data, employee performance details, compensation data, emails, CRM exports or sensitive commercial information into any external tool unless approved and safe.
original_action: Create columns: owner, ask, deadline, blocked decision, risk, last chase, next chase, escalation date, status. Add the five most urgent open asks.
